folders.name: hammock-sleep-health
folders.original_query: health impact of sleeping in a hammock full time

files.summary.md:
# Hammock Sleep Health Research

## Key Findings

Sleeping full-time in a hammock can improve spinal alignment due to the diagonal lay position. A Brazilian-style hammock allows the sleeper to lie at approximately 30 degrees to the centerline, flattening the back. Studies from the University of Geneva suggest hammock rocking accelerates sleep onset and deep sleep phases. However, long-term full-time use may cause lower back issues if the hammock is not properly sized or hung.

## Sources

- University of Geneva sleep study (2011)
- Brazilian hammock ergonomics paper (2018)
- Mayo Clinic: Sleep posture guidelines
files.notes.md:
## Research Notes

- Brazilian vs Mayan vs Nicaraguan hammock styles
- Diagonal lay angle: 30 degrees optimal
- Hammock width recommendations: 11ft for taller people, 9-10ft for average
- Back pain reports from improper hang angles (< 30 degrees)
- Rocking motion: 0.2 Hz frequency optimal for sleep induction
- Temperature regulation: hammocks sleep cooler than beds

folders.name: hammock-sizing-guide
folders.original_query: what size hammock for tall person 6ft 5in

files.findings.md:
# Hammock Sizing for Tall People

## Recommendations

For someone 6ft 5in (196cm), an 11ft hammock is the minimum recommended length. A 12ft hammock provides even more comfort. The ridgeline length should be approximately 83% of the hammock length (9.1ft for an 11ft hammock).

## Weight Considerations

- Single layer: up to 250 lbs
- Double layer: up to 400 lbs
- For tall individuals, double-layer provides less stretch and more support.

## Fabric

- 1.6 oz HyperD for lightweight
- 1.9 oz ripstop nylon for durability
- 2.2 oz Hex70 for heavy-duty

folders.name: hammock-camping-gear
folders.original_query: best hammock camping setup for backpacking

files.gear-list.md:
# Hammock Camping Gear List

## Essential Gear

- 11ft hammock with bug net (Dream Hammock Darien, Warbonnet Blackbird)
- Underquilt rated to 20F (Hammock Gear Incubator)
- Top quilt rated to 20F (Enlightened Equipment Revelation)
- Tarp with doors (Warbonnet Superfly, Hammock Gear Journey)
- Tree straps (1 inch minimum, 12ft length)
- Whoopie slings or cinch buckles for suspension

## Weight Breakdown

Total base weight: 3.2 lbs (hammock + tarp + quilts + suspension)

folders.name: standing-desk-ergonomics
folders.original_query: best standing desk setup for productivity and back health

files.summary.md:
# Standing Desk Ergonomics

## Key Recommendations

- Desk height: elbows at 90 degrees when typing
- Monitor: top of screen at eye level, 20-28 inches away
- Anti-fatigue mat recommended for prolonged standing
- Sit-stand ratio: 1:1 or 2:1 (sit:stand)
- Standing duration: no more than 2 hours at a time initially

## Health Benefits

- Reduced lower back pain (52% improvement in studies)
- Increased calorie burn: 170 extra calories per 3 hours standing
- Improved posture and core engagement

folders.name: coffee-brewing-methods
folders.original_query: comparison of pour over vs french press vs espresso

files.comparison.md:
# Coffee Brewing Methods Comparison

## Pour Over (V60)
- Grind: medium-fine (sea salt texture)
- Brew time: 3-4 minutes
- Flavor profile: clean, bright, highlights acidity
- Best for: light to medium roasts

## French Press
- Grind: coarse (sea salt)
- Brew time: 4 minutes
- Flavor profile: full-bodied, rich, oils preserved
- Best for: medium to dark roasts

## Espresso
- Grind: very fine
- Brew time: 25-30 seconds
- Flavor profile: concentrated, intense, crema layer
- Best for: all roast levels (espresso-specific roasts)

folders.name: sleep-optimization
folders.original_query: how to optimize sleep quality with temperature light and schedule

files.README.md:
# Sleep Optimization Research Plan

## Goal
Research evidence-based methods for optimizing sleep quality through environmental and behavioral factors.

## Research Questions
1. What is the optimal bedroom temperature for sleep?
2. How does blue light exposure affect melatonin and sleep onset?
3. What sleep schedules are supported by circadian rhythm research?
4. Does napping improve or impair nighttime sleep quality?

## Passes
1. Broad: temperature and sleep quality meta-analyses
2. Primary: blue light and melatonin suppression studies
3. Independent: circadian rhythm and shift work research
4. Failure: limitations of sleep hygiene interventions
files.findings.md:
## Temperature

Optimal bedroom temperature: 60-67 degrees F (15.5-19.4 C). Temperatures above 75 F (24 C) increase wakefulness and reduce REM sleep. A warm bath 1-2 hours before bed paradoxically lowers core body temperature and improves sleep onset.

## Light

Blue light (460-480 nm) suppresses melatonin by up to 50% with just 2 hours of evening exposure. Amber/red light after sunset preserves melatonin. Morning bright light exposure (10,000 lux for 30 min) anchors circadian rhythm.

## Schedule

Consistent wake time is more important than consistent bed time. The sleep-wake homeostat and circadian process interact: sleep pressure builds during wakefulness, while circadian alerting signal opposes it in the evening.

folders.name: ergonomic-chair-buying-guide
folders.original_query: best ergonomic office chair under 1000 dollars

files.recommendations.md:
# Ergonomic Chair Recommendations Under $1000

## Top Picks

1. **Steelcase Leap V2 (used/refurbished)** - $500-800
   - LiveBack technology, adjustable lumbar, 4D armrests
2. **Herman Miller Aeron (used)** - $600-900
   - PostureFit SL, Pellicle mesh, three sizes (A/B/C)
3. **Haworth Fern** - $800-1000
   - Digital knit back, adjustable lumbar bubble

## What to Look For

- Adjustable seat depth
- Lumbar support (adjustable height + firmness)
- 4D armrests
- Forward tilt for task work
- Mesh or fabric (personal preference)

folders.name: protein-intake-muscle
folders.original_query: optimal daily protein intake for muscle growth per kg bodyweight

files.notes.md:
## Protein Intake Research

### Recommendations
- 1.6-2.2 g/kg/day for muscle hypertrophy (ISSN position stand)
- Upper end (2.2 g/kg) for cutting/caloric deficit
- Lower end (1.6 g/kg) for maintenance/bulking

### Timing
- 20-40g protein per meal for maximal MPS stimulation
- Leucine threshold: 2-3g per meal
- Pre-sleep casein: 30-40g improves overnight MPS

### Sources
- Whey: fast absorption, high leucine
- Casein: slow absorption, anti-catabolic
- Plant: combine sources for complete EAA profile

### Studies
- Schoenfeld et al. (2018) meta-analysis: 1.6 g/kg plateau
- Morton et al. (2018): 1.6 g/kg for lean mass gains
- Phillips & Van Loon (2011): 1.2-1.7 g/kg for athletes
